description: Fires when a visitor moves the cursor away from the current page. By default the trigger fires on all pages; add conditions to fire only on a specific page or under specific criteria. You must specify when the trigger fires: cursor leaves via the upper page boundary (exit intent) or via upper, bottom or side boundary, using the built-in Leave Content variable. See Piwik PRO help: Page leave trigger.

leave_content_condition.description: Fires the tag when a visitor moves the cursor in the selected direction
options: Upper, bottom or side page boundary

builtin_variable.name: Leave Content
builtin_variable.description: A variable used in the page leave trigger. It defines which cursor movement triggers the tag.
builtin_variable.usage: Use this variable_id in the condition that defines when the trigger fires. The condition's value must be one of: 'top' (upper page boundary / exit intent), 'any' (upper, bottom or side page boundary).

value_descriptions.top: Upper page boundary (exit intent) – fires when the cursor leaves via the top of the viewport
value_descriptions.any: Upper, bottom or side page boundary – fires when the cursor leaves via any edge

multiplicity: Many times during a session (each time)
leave_content_conditions_reference.note: The leave-content condition must use the built-in variable 'Leave Content' (id c2fe42d6-2cbc-45d0-b4df-af92fdbdc2fc). Set the condition value to 'top' for exit intent only, or 'any' for any boundary. Multiplicity (each time, once per page view, once per session) is configured via this variable/trigger settings.

condition_types.description: Fires when a visitor moves the cursor off a page with the selected page URL
Fires when a visitor moves the cursor off a page with the selected page path
Fires when a visitor moves the cursor off a page hosted on the selected domain
Fires when a visitor moves the cursor off a page to which they were directed by the selected internal referrer
Fires when a visitor moves the cursor off a page to which they were directed by the selected external referrer
Fires when a visitor moves the cursor off a page to which they were directed by the selected campaign (utm_campaign)
Fires when a visitor moves the cursor off a page to which they were directed by the selected traffic source. Values: campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct
Fires when a visitor is a returning visitor and moves the cursor off the page
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

when_to_use: Exit-intent popups or overlays (use value 'top' for upper boundary only)
Tracking or messaging when the user shows intent to leave the page
Firing when the cursor leaves any edge of the page (use value 'any')
common_use_cases: Exit-intent offer or newsletter signup modal
Leave-content analytics or tracking on key pages
best_practices: Use 'top' for classic exit intent (cursor leaves upward); use 'any' to catch any leave direction
Add Page path or Page URL conditions to limit the trigger to specific pages
Consider multiplicity: once per page view or once per session to avoid repeated popups

create_trigger.description: Create a page leave (leave content) trigger. Requires at least one condition using the built-in 'Leave Content' variable (variable_id c2fe42d6-2cbc-45d0-b4df-af92fdbdc2fc) with value 'top' or 'any'; optionally add event conditions (Page URL, Page path, etc.) and configure multiplicity (each time, once per page view, once per session).

trigger_type.description: Must be 'leave_content' for page leave triggers

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Include at least one condition with variable_id c2fe42d6-2cbc-45d0-b4df-af92fdbdc2fc (Leave Content) and value 'top' or 'any'; optional event conditions for Page URL, Page path, Referrer, etc. Multiplicity is configured per condition/trigger (each time, once per page view, once per session).

update_trigger.description: Update an existing page leave trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include at least one condition using the built-in variable 'Leave Content' (variable_id c2fe42d6-2cbc-45d0-b4df-af92fdbdc2fc) with value 'top' (upper boundary / exit intent) or 'any' (upper, bottom or side). May include event conditions: Page URL, Page path, Page hostname, Referrer, External referrer, Campaign, Traffic source, Returning visitor, or variable-based. The Leave Content variable supports multiplicity: each time, once per page view, or once per session.

structure.variable_id: For leave boundary use c2fe42d6-2cbc-45d0-b4df-af92fdbdc2fc (Leave Content); for event conditions use the appropriate variable UUID
structure.condition_type: equals (use for Leave Content value); other types for event conditions
structure.value: When using Leave Content variable: 'top' (exit intent) or 'any' (any boundary)

conditions.recommendation: Include one condition with variable_id c2fe42d6-2cbc-45d0-b4df-af92fdbdc2fc (Leave Content) and value 'top' or 'any'; add Page path or Page URL conditions to limit where the trigger fires.

leave_content_exit_intent.description: Fire on exit intent (cursor leaves via upper boundary) on all pages

leave_content_any_boundary.description: Fire when cursor leaves via any boundary (top, bottom or side)

leave_content_with_page_path.description: Exit intent only on product pages

common_mistakes: Using a value other than 'top' or 'any' for the Leave Content variable
Omitting the Leave Content condition so the trigger does not know which boundary to use
Expecting 'top' to fire when the cursor leaves via bottom or sides (use 'any' for that)

possible_causes: Leave Content condition value not 'top' or 'any'
Multiplicity set to once per session and already fired
debugging_steps: Verify one condition uses variable_id c2fe42d6-2cbc-45d0-b4df-af92fdbdc2fc with value 'top' or 'any'
Test with only the Leave Content condition
Check multiplicity (each time vs once per page view vs once per session)
